risks.description: Significant investments in renewable energy and carbon tax mitigation are required, with immature commercial solutions increasing alignment costs.
mitigation: EUR 32.4m (2024: EUR 23.2m) OPEX for carbon tax mitigation

risks.description: Immature commercial or technical alternatives delay full adoption of renewable energy and emissions reduction levers.

risks.risk_title: Slow EV fleet transition in service operations
risks.description: Only 25% (down from 26%) of vehicles are renewably fuelled by end-2025, despite targets for regional EV adoption.
mitigation: Increased charging infrastructure (160+ chargers in Denmark)

risks.description: Scope 3 GHG target of 45% per MWh by 2030 requires supplier collaboration; progress depends on market-based solutions.

risks.risk_title: Climate policy uncertainty affecting global targets
risks.description: Industry may struggle to meet climate goals without supportive policies, risking operational alignment delays.
Pilot vessel (2025–2026) relies on evolving methanol standards; operational updates may extend timeline.
mitigation: 10-year charter agreement with Northern Offshore Service

risks.risk_title: Renewable energy sourcing dependency on policies
risks.description: 100% renewable electricity sourcing depends on supportive policies; lack of them risks operational disruptions.
Future climate risks (e.g., water stress, extreme rainfall) are based on uncertain assumptions; physical impacts may not materialize financially.
mitigation: Value-chain prioritization for direct impact areas
